mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding economic output, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is always active and does not require a threshold.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positively correlated with INDPRO changes (Spearman rank correlation p > 0.05 over any 10-year period).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0% signals labor market weakness that erodes consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where UNRATE is above 5.0%.

mechanism_narrative: When both the federal funds rate and consumer price index are elevated, the economy faces stagflationary pressures that compress equity valuations due to higher discount rates and lower growth expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not significantly negative in periods where both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL are above their respective medians (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In an expansion regime defined by unemployment below 5% and industrial production above 100 index points, rising payroll employment reinforces income and consumption, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The signal is regime-conditional: fails outside expansion.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in expansion regime with rising PAYEMS are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When year-over-year CPI inflation exceeds 3%, the central bank is likely to tighten monetary policy, which increases discount rates and depresses equity prices, yielding negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in at least 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts signal robust consumer and investment demand, which boosts economic growth expectations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days. The interaction amplifies the individual effects.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during periods of simultaneous positive monthly growth in RSAFS and HOUST are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05 compared to all periods).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month industrial production growth exceeding 0.5% indicates immediate supply-side acceleration that markets price into short-term earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in more than half of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales volume signals strengthening consumer spending, which drives corporate revenue and earnings growth, resulting in positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days. The signal operates continuously without a threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS monthly change against 20-day forward returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
